steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content:
Rewrite line $s$ in slope-intercept form:
$$y + 16 = 7x \implies y = 7x - 16$$
Slope of line $s$ = 7, y-intercept = $-16$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel-lines condition
steps.content:
Parallel lines have equal slopes and different y-intercepts. So the slope of line $t$ must equal 7. Test each choice as a possible slope of line $t$:
  • Choice A — $-16$: This is the y-intercept of line $s$, not its slope. Slope $-16 eq 7$. ✗
  • Choice B — $-7$: Slope $-7 eq 7$. A line with slope $-7$ would be perpendicular in sign but not parallel. ✗
  • Choice C — $7$: Slope $7 = 7$. Matches the slope of line $s$. ✓
  • Choice D — $16$: Slope $16 eq 7$. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: The slope of any line parallel to $y = 7x - 16$ is 7. That matches choice C — C is correct.
